name: Marqo
tagline: Vector search over text and images with embeddings built in
seoTitle: Marqo - Vector Search Engine for Text and Images
metaDescription: Marqo is an open-source vector search engine that generates and stores embeddings from text and images, so you can run semantic search without a separate model step.
overview: Marqo is an open-source vector search engine. Unlike a plain vector database, it handles the embedding step for you: you send raw text or images, and Marqo generates the vectors, stores them, and serves semantic search over them. This means you don't have to run a separate model to turn your documents into vectors before indexing.

It runs as a Docker container with an HTTP API and a Python client. You create an index, add documents, and search with a natural-language query. Marqo decides which documents are the closest match by meaning rather than by exact keyword overlap, which makes it a fit for the vector-database category where retrieval quality depends on good embeddings.

Note: the Marqo open-source project is marked as deprecated in its README and no longer receives updates. The company now points new users to its hosted product at marqo.ai. The open-source code remains usable for self-hosted projects, but plan around the lack of ongoing maintenance.
features: Generates embeddings from text and images at index time, so no separate embedding pipeline is needed
Runs as a single Docker container that exposes an HTTP API
Official Python client for creating indexes, adding documents, and searching
Tensor fields let you choose which document fields are embedded for semantic search
Returns ranked results by semantic similarity to a natural-language query

gettingStarted.intro: Marqo runs as a Docker container and is driven from Python. Start the server, install the client, then index and search a few documents.

steps.heading: Start the Marqo server with Docker
steps.body: Pull and run the Marqo image. It listens on port 8882.
steps.code:
docker rm -f marqo
docker pull marqoai/marqo:latest
docker run --name marqo -p 8882:8882 marqoai/marqo:latest

steps.heading: Install the Python client
steps.body: Install the marqo package from PyPI.

steps.heading: Index documents and search
steps.body: Connect to the local server, create an index, add documents with tensor_fields set to the field you want embedded, then run a natural-language query.

useCases: Semantic search over a document collection without building a separate embedding pipeline
Image search where queries and stored images are matched by meaning rather than tags
Adding natural-language retrieval to an app prototype using a single self-hosted container
Backing a retrieval step for question-answering over your own text data
